name: Resonate
description: Resonator module (clone of Mutable Instruments Rings). Transforms external excitation audio into pitched sound via modal, sympathetic string, or non-linear string physical modeling. Mono-to-poly polyphony with strumming.

controls.name: Polyphony Setting
controls.description: Selects monophonic, duophonic, or quadraphonic operation. More voices overlap without cutting tails.

controls.name: Resonator Type
controls.description: Selects resonator model: modal (bars/plates), sympathetic strings (comb filters), or non-linear/inharmonic string (extended Karplus-Strong).

controls.name: Frequency (Coarse)
controls.description: Coarse frequency control. Quantized in semitones when a cable is patched to the V/OCT input.
controls.detail: Quantized in semitones when V/OCT patched

controls.name: Structure
controls.description: Harmonic structure. Frequency ratio between partials (modal), detuning of strings (sympathetic), or non-linearity/inharmonicity (string).

controls.name: Brightness
controls.description: Adjusts level of higher harmonics. Acts simultaneously on LPF of exciter (closed at 8 o'clock) and damping filter on higher modes.

controls.name: Damping
controls.description: Controls the decay time of the sound.

controls.name: Position
controls.description: Excitation position on the string/surface. Similar to PWM on a square oscillator or comb-filtering of a phaser.

controls.name: Attenuverters (4x)
controls.description: Attenuverters for the CV inputs (Frequency, Structure, Brightness, Position/Damping).
controls.detail: Bipolar · FREQUENCY CV normalized to small constant for fine tune use

inputs.name: STRUM
inputs.description: Strumming trigger input. Freezes current voice, lets it decay, starts next voice. Normalized to step detector on V/OCT and transient detector on IN.
inputs.normalizedTo: V/OCT step detector / IN transient detector

inputs.name: V/OCT
inputs.description: 1V/octave CV pitch control for main resonator frequency.

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Audio excitation signal input. Normalized to internal pulse/burst generator that reacts to V/OCT changes or STRUM triggers.

inputs.name: Frequency CV
inputs.description: CV input for Frequency parameter. Normalized to small constant allowing attenuverter as fine tune when unpatched.

outputs.name: ODD
outputs.description: Odd output. In mono: odd-numbered partials (modal) or one picking position (string). In poly: odd-numbered voices. Mix of both if EVEN is unpatched.

outputs.name: EVEN
outputs.description: Even output. In mono: even-numbered partials or complementary component. In poly: even-numbered voices. Must be patched to split signals.

behaviors.name: Modal Resonator
behaviors.description: Simulates resonance of vibrating structures (bars, plates, strings) using a bank of band-pass filters. Structure sets frequency ratio between partials.

behaviors.name: Sympathetic Strings
behaviors.description: Simulates strings that vibrate in response to other strings using comb filters. Structure sets tuning ratio between the strings for extra overtones.

behaviors.name: Non-linear/Inharmonic String
behaviors.description: Extended Karplus-Strong with non-linearities and dispersion all-pass filter. Structure sets degree of inharmonicity.

behaviors.name: Internal Excitation
behaviors.description: When IN is unpatched, the module generates its own excitation signal (LP-filtered pulse or noise burst depending on resonator type) on each note change or STRUM.

behaviors.name: Polyphonic Strumming
behaviors.description: In 2 or 4-voice polyphony, successive notes overlap without cutting each other's tails. Rapidly send different pitches to create chords by strumming.

patchIdeas.name: Self-contained melodic resonator
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer into V/OCT only — Resonate generates its own exciter on each note change.
steps: Set Resonator Type to Modal.
Set Polyphony to 1 voice.
DAMPING ~1 o'clock, BRIGHTNESS ~noon, STRUCTURE ~noon, POSITION ~noon.
Leave STRUM and IN unpatched — the internal exciter fires on V/OCT step changes.
Patch sequencer pitch CV → V/OCT.
Patch ODD → mixer input.
patchIdeas.signalOut: ODD — mono modal-resonator audio (±5V range) with internal mallet exciter.
patchIdeas.hear: A plucked metallic/wooden bar that retriggers on every new note from the sequencer. No gate cable needed.
patchIdeas.diagram:
RESONATE:
* Resonator Type: Modal
* Polyphony: 1 voice
- Sequencer (CV) p> Resonate (V/OCT) // step changes trigger internal excitation
- Resonate (ODD) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Drum hits as exciter
patchIdeas.patch: Drum transients feed IN so Resonate becomes a pitched resonant body tuned by V/OCT.
steps: Set Resonator Type to Modal.
DAMPING ~10 o'clock (short decay so resonance tails don't smear the groove).
BRIGHTNESS ~1 o'clock, STRUCTURE ~11 o'clock.
Patch a drum / click source → IN. (Patching IN disables the internal exciter.)
Patch sequencer pitch CV → V/OCT — each hit rings at the sequenced note.
Leave STRUM unpatched — IN transients auto-trigger new voices.
Patch ODD → mixer L, EVEN → mixer R for stereo.
patchIdeas.signalOut: ODD / EVEN — resonated drum audio, pitched by V/OCT, stereo-complementary.
patchIdeas.hear: Drums turn into tuned tom/bell hybrids that follow your melody. Each transient excites the modal bank at the current pitch.
patchIdeas.diagram:
RESONATE:
* Resonator Type: Modal
- Drum (Out) -> Resonate (IN) // percussive exciter
- Sequencer (CV) p> Resonate (V/OCT)
- Resonate (ODD) -> Mixer (In)
- Resonate (EVEN) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Polyphonic chords
patchIdeas.patch: 4-voice polyphony with a fast pitch sequence strums chords from the internal exciter.
steps: Set Resonator Type to Sympathetic strings (for piano/harp character) or Modal.
Set Polyphony to 4 voices.
DAMPING ~2 o'clock, BRIGHTNESS ~2 o'clock, STRUCTURE ~1 o'clock.
Patch an arpeggiator / rapid pitch sequencer → V/OCT. Each step freezes the previous voice and fires a new one.
Leave STRUM unpatched (V/OCT step detector does the strumming). IN unpatched so internal exciter fires.
Patch ODD → mixer L, EVEN → mixer R for stereo chord spread.
patchIdeas.signalOut: ODD / EVEN — up to 4 overlapping voices split into odd/even strings. Decay shortens as voices stack.
patchIdeas.hear: Plucked chords bloom as each sequencer step adds a new voice on top of the decaying previous one.
patchIdeas.diagram:
RESONATE:
* Polyphony: 4 voices
- Sequencer (CV) p> Resonate (V/OCT) // rapid notes strum chord
- Resonate (ODD) -> Mixer (In)
- Resonate (EVEN) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Granular exciter chamber
patchIdeas.patch: uBurst granular cloud drives IN as a continuous bowing-like exciter for sympathetic strings.
steps: Set Resonator Type to Sympathetic strings.
STRUCTURE past noon (wider detuning between strings), DAMPING high (long sustain).
BRIGHTNESS ~1 o'clock, POSITION ~noon.
Patch uBurst (Left Out) → Resonate IN — the grain cloud replaces the internal mallet.
Patch a slow LFO → uBurst Position CV for small grain motion.
Patch a quantized sequencer → Resonate V/OCT.
Patch ODD → mixer L, EVEN → mixer R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: ODD / EVEN — sympathetic strings continuously excited by granular cloud, stereo.
patchIdeas.hear: A bowed sitar-like sustain where grains keep exciting the string bank. Evolving overtones as STRUCTURE detunes.
patchIdeas.diagram:
RESONATE:
* Resonator Type: Sympathetic strings
* Structure: past noon · Damping: high
- uBurst (Left Out) -> Resonate (IN) // granular cloud as continuous exciter
- LFO (Out) >> uBurst (Position CV) // small grain motion
- Quantizer (CV) p> Resonate (V/OCT)
- Resonate (ODD) -> Mixer (In L)
- Resonate (EVEN) -> Mixer (In R)

patchIdeas.name: DV tail as sympathetic resonance
patchIdeas.patch: Desmodus Versio reverb tail excites IN while density CV shapes Damping — reverb becomes pitched string resonance.
steps: Set Resonator Type to Sympathetic strings.
STRUCTURE ~10 o'clock, DAMPING high as a starting point, BRIGHTNESS ~noon.
Patch Desmodus Versio (Out L) → Resonate IN — the wet tail drives the strings.
Patch a density-envelope CV from uBurst → Resonate Damping CV; set the attenuverter to taste.
Patch a slow quantized sequencer → Resonate V/OCT.
Leave STRUM unpatched.
Patch ODD → mixer L, EVEN → mixer R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: ODD / EVEN — reverb tail re-pitched as sympathetic strings, Damping CV'd in real time.
patchIdeas.hear: A lush reverb that slowly blooms into pitched sitar-like harmonics, the decay breathing with the density envelope.
patchIdeas.diagram:
RESONATE:
* Resonator Type: Sympathetic strings
* Structure: ~10 o'clock · Damping: high
- Desmodus Versio (Out L) -> Resonate (IN) // wet reverb tail as exciter
- uBurst (Density Env) >> Resonate (Damping CV) // density shapes decay
- Slow Quant Seq (CV) p> Resonate (V/OCT)
- Resonate (ODD) -> Mixer (In L)
- Resonate (EVEN) -> Mixer (In R)

patchIdeas.name: Resonate + uBurst infinite drone loop
patchIdeas.patch: Closed feedback between Resonate and uBurst — envelope-follower self-plucks on cloud peaks for an infinite drone.
steps: Set Resonator Type to Modal, DAMPING high, BRIGHTNESS ~2 o'clock.
Patch a slow sequencer → Resonate V/OCT.
Patch Resonate ODD → uBurst IN L.
Patch uBurst Out L → envelope follower → Resonate STRUM (peaks re-pluck Resonate).
Patch uBurst Out R → attenuator (start fully CCW) → uBurst Freeze CV for self-latching texture.
Raise the feedback attenuator slowly — too loud and the loop runs away.
Patch uBurst Out R → main output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: uBurst Out R — evolving drone cloud continuously re-excited by Resonate peaks.
patchIdeas.hear: An eternal metallic cloud that feeds itself: each grain peak re-strikes Resonate, whose output re-feeds uBurst. Sequencer pitch nudges the drone's center.
patchIdeas.diagram:
RESONATE:
* Resonator Type: Modal · Damping: high
- Sequencer (CV) p> Resonate (V/OCT)
- Resonate (ODD) -> uBurst (IN L)
- uBurst (Out L) -> Env Follower (In)
- Env Follower (Out) t> Resonate (STRUM) // self-plucking on cloud peaks
- uBurst (Out R) -> Attenuator (In)
- Attenuator (Out) >> uBurst (Freeze CV) // self-latching freeze
- uBurst (Out R) -> Output (In)
